content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

The amendment correctly identifies that the `result` Nix symlink is tracked by git and appears in diffs. While `result` is already in .gitignore, it was committed before that entry was added, so git continues tracking it. The fix requires `git rm --cached result` to untrack it. This is a valid hygiene issue — committing machine-local Nix store paths pollutes the branch.

## Votes

- `fr-3-05f23d9b`: Accept
  Verified: `result` is a symlink to `/nix/store/...` that was committed in 18abf13 before the .gitignore entry existed. Despite `.gitignore` now listing `result`, git continues tracking it because gitignore only affects untracked files. The symlink changed between recent commits (visible in diff). This is a real issue — machine-local Nix store paths should not be in version control. The fix is `git rm --cached result` to stop tracking the already-committed file, after which the existing .gitignore entry will prevent re-addition.
